title: Silky Ajiaco Stir-fry
description: An earthy and silky Argentinian dish featuring dried mint to aid in hangover recovery. Kasher-friendly and suitable for breakfast or dinner.

ingredients.name: Dried Mint
ingredients.notes: Approximately half the amount of fresh mint

ingredients.name: Garlic Clove(s)
ingredients.notes: Minced or minced into a fine paste for added depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Tomato Paste
ingredients.notes: To add richness and color to the dish

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: For sautéing vegetables and adding richness to the stir-fry

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: For seasoning the dish, use low-sodium if available

instructions: Heat olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add onion and garlic to the pan. Sauté until onions are translucent.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for about 1 minute until it thickens slightly.
Crush dried mint finely and add to the pan, stirring well to release its flavor.
Return chicken to the wok or skillet, ensuring all pieces are coated with the sauce.
Add soy sauce, cornstarch dissolved in a little water (to avoid clumping), and cook until mixture thickens slightly.
Garnish with fresh mint leaves before serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
